title: Zollikoferia fallax
intro: Zollikoferia fallax, also known as False Buttonweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant species native to South America. Traditionally, Zollikoferia fallax has been used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Zollikoferia fallax include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Zollikoferia fallax is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Zollikoferia fallax is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is native to South America. The plant's classification is primarily based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf structure, flower morphology, and seed features. As a part of the Fabaceae family, Zollikoferia fallax is a legume, meaning it has the ability to fix nitrogen from the air. Further classification details, such as its genus and species relationships, can be found in botanical databases and scientific literature.
